首页.text:
2025 WINNERS
NEWS
PARTNERS
CATEGORIES
JURY 2026
GALLERY 
CONTACT 
ARCHIVE 

For 20 years, the Sustainability Awards have set the benchmark for excellence in Australia’s built environment — recognising projects, organisations and individuals delivering measurable, lasting sustainability impact. Long before sustainability became standard practice, the Awards championed leadership, innovation and credibility.

Now celebrating its 20th anniversary, we remain Australia’s longest-running and most respected program dedicated to sustainable building — trusted by industry and recognised by leaders shaping the future of the built environment.

Entries open 29 April 2026.

VIEW THE 2025 WINNERS

Partners

We’re partnering with like-minded brands who share our vision for change. Together we’re supporting the ongoing progress of the green building movement in our great nation.

Partner with us
Our packages are meticulously crafted to position our Partners as the premier solution providers within their sponsored categories. This association aligns their brand with award-winning architectural projects and prominent professionals within the Architecture and Design network. Additionally, Partners receive exclusive access to valuable networking opportunities at the Sustainability Summit and Awards gala evening.

For more information about partner opportunities that may remain available in 2025, please contact Adrian at adrian.wilson@architectureanddesign.com.au or complete the contact enquiry form below.

获奖者.text:
Home > 2025 Winners2025 Winners
2025 Sustainability Awards Winners

On November 19, Sydney’s iconic Town Hall hosted the Sustainability Awards Gala, uniting over 300 attendees, including jurors, partners, and industry leaders. The night celebrated innovation and connection, highlighting the industry’s unwavering commitment to sustainability.

Over 250 outstanding projects, people, and products were submitted, making the selection of finalists a challenging task for our expert jury.

Explore the 2025 Winners below.

类别.text:
Home > 2026 Categories2026 Categories
Categories

The Sustainability Awards is Australia’s longest-running and most prestigious awards program dedicated to acknowledging and celebrating excellence in sustainable design and architecture.

People
Emerging Sustainable Architect/Designer
Open to Australian-based architects/designers in their first five years of their career and recognises an outstanding achievement or development in the field of sustainable and / or environmental architecture planning or design across any of the building categories.
Lifetime Achievement

A person who has over their career, shown exemplary efforts in advancing the progression of sustainability in Australia.

Sustainable Builder/Developer of the Year
Recognising built environment sector leading companies driving excellence in new projects that integrate environmental performance, social responsibility, and long-term value creation, as well as measurable sustainability outcomes.
Projects
Commercial

Class 5, 6, 7 or 8 buildings designed and/or modified to be sustainable for the purpose of accommodating a range of business activities, highlighting innovative design, materials, and technologies that reduce environmental impact while enhancing occupant wellbeing.

Community & Social Impact
Celebrating designs that create lasting positive change for people and communities, empowering individuals, promoting social equality, and strengthening community resilience, while also improving the quality of community life.
Institutional & Public Buildings
Class 9 buildings pushing boundaries in sustainable design and operation for civic, educational, and institutional entities, while also demonstrating energy efficiency, resource stewardship, and overall social responsibility.
Multi Residential Dwelling
The design of a new townhouse, duplex or residential unit complex that contains multiple residential dwellings. Projects nominated under this category are classifiable as Class 2 or Class 3 buildings.
Single Residential Dwelling

New, renovated, and rebuilt Class 1 residential buildings that exemplify sustainable design, while minimising environmental impacts using innovative designs, materials, and technologies to reduce environmental impact while enhancing occupant wellbeing.

International Design

Awarded to the best environmentally responsible project submitted from outside of Australia.

Adaptive Reuse
Recognises the adaptive reuse of a building (heritage and/or new) that has minimal impact on any historical significance of the building and its setting, while at the same time, pursuing a design that is sympathetic to the building in order to give it a new purpose and/or use.
Landscape & Urban
A building or amenity design which primarily services, or is used, by the public (except educational facilities) that adheres and promotes to the concepts of sustainability, renewability and regenerative design.
Interior Design
A renovation or fit-out of the interior of an existing building where the sustainable design is involved with such factors as determining the efficient and effective use of space, selecting construction materials that offer low environmental impact and lowering pollution, waste, and lowering the overall energy consumption.

 

Hospitality & Tourism
Examples of Hospitality & Tourism Design include projects that help environmentally friendly operation, education and awareness programs, energy and resource conservation, waste & carbon dioxide reduction, heritage protected sites and adaptive reuse. The types of buildings includes but is not limited to hotels, pubs, bars, motels, spas, resorts, restaurants, cafes, breweries and anywhere where entertainment, food and/or beverages are provided to the public.
Prefab & Modular

Prefabricated and/or modular structures or modules built faster off-site with less waste, less labour, and with the finished product being quantifiably high in quality and sustainability.

 

 

 

Innovation
Green Building Material/Solution
A building product, material, product or solution that is renewable, environmentally responsible and where the impact is considered over the entire lifetime of the product.
Sustainable Architectural Practice Of The Year
Recognising firms leading the way in environmental and socially responsible design with a consistent track record of integrating sustainability across project typologies, as well as the practice’s inherent culture that promotes a more sustainable built environment.
Editor’s Choice

Editor’s Choice is an award given by the Architecture & Design editorial team. All shortlisted projects are assessed for their suitability for this award on the basis of their contribution to and consultation with their local community.

Best of the Best

Selected from among all the category winners on the basis of both sustainable outcomes and aesthetics and/or innovative design principles.

Need more information?

VIEW CATEGORY CRITERIA

Have questions?

VIEW FAQ

评审.text:
Home > Jury 2026Jury 2026
2026 Jury

They’re leaders in their respective fields. They’re from universities, industry associations and architecture firms, and they know what sustainable buildings look like. They are world-class industry experts and world-class judges, and will ensure that only the country’s most pioneering green projects have made the final cut.

Ben Peake

Principal, Carter Williamson Architects


In 2023, Ben was awarded the National Emerging Architect Prize for his outstanding contribution to architectural practice, education, design excellence, and community involvement. This is evidenced through his involvement as a founding member of Save Our Sirius, and Architecture With Pride. Ben is also a recipient of the 2019 Dulux Study Tour Prize, the 2020 Adrian Ashton Prize for Architectural Culture and Literature.

Significant recent Carter Williamson projects by Ben include the award-winning Woodcroft Neighbourhood Centre for Blacktown City Council, the seductive Concrete Blonde, and a sculptural waterfront home in Birchgrove in Sydney’s Inner West.

Dick Clarke

Founding Director, Envirotecture
Australia

Dick Clarke, with 45 years of experience, is the principal of Envirotecture, specialising in sustainable and culturally appropriate buildings.

He has held significant roles in various sustainability organisations and authored key publications like the “Your Home” series.

Dominika Richards

Sustainability Lead, Deicke Richards


Dominika is a registered architect and Sustainability Lead at Deicke Richards, with experience across affordable housing, education, health and community projects. She champions socially and ecologically sustainable design, co-chairs the AIA Qld Climate and Sustainability Action Group, facilitates the Living Design Series, and actively contributes to industry forums and climate initiatives.

Gabrielle Pavicic

Green Star Program Lead, Green Building Council of Australia


Gabrielle Pavicic is Green Star Program Lead at the Green Building Council of Australia, specialising in sustainable design across the built environment. With a background in architecture and interiors, she leads major rating tools and national initiatives, and was named Emerging Sustainable Architect/Designer at the 2025 Sustainability Awards

Gagendeep Jadhav

Sustainability Consultant, Steensen Varming


Gagandeep Jadhav is a Sustainability Consultant at Steensen Varming based in Sydney. He works closely with architects, engineers and developers to deliver high-performance, environmentally responsible buildings. Gagandeep specialises in ESD strategies, building performance and green building certifications. His work focuses on integrating practical, evidence-based sustainability solutions into the design process to achieve meaningful environmental outcomes.

Heather McCabe

Architect and Certified Passivhaus Designer, DJRD Architects


Heather McCabe is an Architect and Certified Passivhaus Designer at DJRD Architects in Sydney. Heather is leading the development of DJRD’s Sustainability Action Plan and is implementing project sustainability strategies across the practice. Heather has previously served as a Board Member for the Australian Passivhaus Association and is currently involved with the Australian Architects Declare Education team.

Jeremy Spencer

Director, Positive Footprints


Jeremy Spencer has been a registered builder and director at Positive Footprints for nearly 20 years. He is an advocate for sustainable design and construction and serves on multiple industry boards.

Mahalath Halperin

Architect & Director, Mahalath Halperin Architects


Mahalath Halperin, FRAIA, has managed her architectural practice since the 1990s, focusing on sustainable and liveable designs. She also conducts environmental audits and has authored various publications, including children’s books.

Mick Paterson

General Manager, Blaise Building Services


Mick Paterson is a licensed builder with over 30 years of experience across four countries. He specialises in sustainable construction, Passive House, and creating healthy, high-performance buildings. Mick’s passion for green building began over two decades ago, leading to diverse residential and commercial work, including collaborations with Passive House organisations like Indwell and Invisij in Canada. He is currently the General Manager of Blaise Building Services, a Passive House-only builder based in Sydney’s eastern suburbs. Mick is committed to crafting spaces that are both environmentally responsible and conducive to well-being.

Sandra Furtado

Director, Furtado Sullivan


Sandra is co-founder of Furtado Sullivan, a Sydney architecture studio known for merging large-scale efficiency with bespoke design. She has contributed to major projects including Barangaroo and One Circular Quay. Sandra excels in navigating urban complexity, using holistic design thinking and multidisciplinary collaboration to deliver creative, sustainable outcomes.

Shae Parker McCashen

Founding Director, Green Sheep Collective


Shae Parker McCashen is the founding director of Green Sheep Collective, a Melbourne-based practice focused on sustainable, community-driven design. With over 24 years of industry experience, she is a passionate advocate for passive solar design, detail-oriented architecture and environmental responsibility. Shae regularly contributes to industry discourse through writing, speaking, and leadership roles within the architectural community.

SHARE

关于.text:
Home > About UsAbout Us
About Sustainability Awards

The Sustainability Awards is Australia’s longest-running and most prestigious awards program dedicated to acknowledging and celebrating excellence in sustainable design and architecture. Entries received are reviewed by a panel of judges and winners in 18 categories are announced at the five-star Sustainability Awards gala evening hosted at the Museum of Contemporary Art, Sydney, on 12 November 2026.

About Sustainability Summit

The Sustainability Summit is a day-long formal CPD education program covering the entire sustainability spectrum within the built environment. The Sustainability Summit program is presented by industry experts and professionals who aim to educate, inspire and ignite learning within the Australian Architecture and Design community. The Sustainability Summit will be hosted at the Fullerton Hotel, Sydney, on 12 November 2026.

SHARE

FAQ.text:
Home > FAQ - SummitFAQ – Summit
Sustainability Summit 2026
Frequently Asked Questions
When Is The 2026 Sustainability Summit?

The 2026 Sustainability Summit is on Thursday, 12th November 2026.

Is The Sustainability Summit Online Or In-Person?

The 2026 Sustainability Summit is an in person event only.

The event is being held at The Fullerton Hotel, 1 Martin Place, Sydney, NSW, 2000.

 

How Do I Earn CPD Points At The Sustainability Summit?

If you are an Australian Architect, you are eligible to earn up to 8 formal CPD points with the Sustainability Summit, one for each session you attend.

To register for each CPD point, you will need to scan the QR Code which will be made available to you at the beginning and end of each session you attend in person. You will be prompted to fill out a form to confirm your attendance at each session. Please ensure you check in using the same email address you used to purchase your ticket.

You are also required to download and complete the question sheet for each session, as well as the respective answer sheet for each session to check your answers. The question and answer sheets are available within each session page for each session here: https://www.sustainablebuildingawards.com.au/summit/

You will be sent your CPD certificate by email within five working days of your attendance. Your certificate will be sent to the email account you registered when purchasing your ticket.

 

Where Can I Find The Question And Answer Sheet Required To Receive A CPD Point For The Sessions I Have Attended?
These will be made available on the sustainability Summit website.
Q&A sheets for each session can be downloaded from the relevant session page.
How And When Will I Receive My CPD Certificate?

If you have watched one of the Sustainability Summit sessions and are enquiring about receiving your certificate, please ensure:

Your CPD certificate/s will be emailed to you. Please allow up to 5 business days from when you attend/view to receive your certificate.
You are on the lookout for an email sent from editor@architectureanddesign.com.au.
You check your junk inbox.

If you receive your certificate but are unsure about how to download/save the file, please follow the steps below:

OPTION 1: SAVE AS PDF
Right click the ‘view & print browser’, copy/open link in new web page
File > Print > Save as PDF
If the Sustainability Summit logo is not showing, your web browser may have an ad blocker so use an alternative web browser (e.g. try Safari)
Change page size: A4
If the page goes over 2 pages, you can change the scale e.g. 75%, 70% etc
Architects manually input their registration number
OPTION 2 (IF YOU ARE HAVING TROUBLE WITH SAVING AS A PDF, YOU MAY SCREENSHOT THE EMAIL CERTIFICATE)

Please keep in mind architects manually input their registration number.

If you still have not received your certificates, please send an email to info@sustainablebuildingawards.com.au with the subject line “Sustainability Summit Missing Certificate”.

How Do I Go Into The Running To Win At The Sustainability Summit?

All ticket holders (digital or in-person) will go into the running to win a prize (Prize to be announced)

Further to this, in-person attendees will also have the chance to win further door prizes when they attend on the day.
